identity.short_name: Mobius inversion on a triple-prime modulus with dual valuation extraction, 2-adic LTE order control, prime-power order lifting, and Hensel square-root counting

problem_spec.summary: On the divisor lattice of $n_0=2^a p^k q^2$, define a valuation-sensitive function $F$ with three additive twists: one active on multiples of $2$, one on multiples of $p$, and one on multiples of $q$. Recover $f(n_0)$ uniquely by Möbius inversion, extract both $t_2=v_2(f(n_0))$ and $t_p=v_p(f(n_0))$ to form the unit $M=f(n_0)/(2^{t_2}p^{t_p})$. Then compute (i) $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ using $2$-adic LTE for the $2^a$ component, LTE-based order lifting for odd prime powers, and CRT-lcm recombination, and (ii) the root count $s$ for $x^2\equiv M$ on the odd part using quadratic reciprocity, Hensel lifting, and CRT counting. Output $r+s$.
givens: $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes; $a\ge 3$ and $k\ge 2$
For each divisor $n\mid n_0$ with $u=v_2(n)$, $i=v_p(n)$, $j=v_q(n)$, define $F(n)=n+C\mathbf{1}_{u\ge 1}\cdot n/2 + B\mathbf{1}_{i\ge 1}\cdot n/p + A\mathbf{1}_{j\ge 1}\cdot n/q$
There exists an arithmetic function $f$ on divisors of $n_0$ such that $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$ for all $n\mid n_0$

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: base odd prime for the $p^k$ component and $p$-adic lifting

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: base odd prime for the $q^2$ component and residue/order computations

parameter(s).name: a
parameter(s).purpose: depth of the $2$-power modulus $2^a$ introducing a 2-adic component in CRT

invariant(s).name: convolution_encoding
invariant(s).statement: $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$ is equivalent to $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ under Dirichlet convolution on divisors of $n_0$

invariant(s).name: mobius_inverse_of_1
invariant(s).statement: $\mu*\mathbf{1}=\varepsilon$ on the divisor lattice, so $f$ is uniquely determined by $f=F*\mu$

invariant(s).name: squarefree_reduction_for_2a_p_k_q2
invariant(s).statement: For $n_0=2^a p^k q^2$, Möbius inversion collapses to the 8-term inclusion-exclusion over squarefree $d\mid 2pq$

invariant(s).name: full_support_evaluation
invariant(s).statement: If $a\ge 3$ and $k\ge 2$ (and $q^2$ fixed), every $n_0/d$ for squarefree $d\mid 2pq$ remains divisible by $2,p,q$, so the full three-twist formula for $F(n_0/d)$ applies uniformly

invariant(s).name: dual_valuation_unit_extraction
invariant(s).statement: Defining $M=f(n_0)/(2^{v_2(f(n_0))}p^{v_p(f(n_0))})$ forces $\gcd(M,2p)=1$; combined with a mod-$q$ check yields $\gcd(M,n_0)=1$

invariant(s).name: odd_prime_order_lifting
invariant(s).statement: LTE-based valuation growth yields exact order lifting from $\operatorname{ord}_\ell(M)$ to $\operatorname{ord}_{\ell^e}(M)$ for odd primes $\ell$

invariant(s).name: quadratic_residue_hensel_invariant
invariant(s).statement: If $\left(\frac{M}{\ell}\right)=1$ for an odd prime $\ell$, then $x^2\equiv M\pmod{\ell^e}$ has exactly 2 solutions for all $e\ge 1$ by Hensel lifting

invariant(s).name: CRT_coupling_shared_config
invariant(s).statement: The same extracted unit $M$ drives two coupled outputs: $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ (via CRT-lcm) and $s$ (via CRT-product of root counts), and the asked quantity is $r+s$

essential_assumption(s): Möbius inversion applies: $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ implies $f=F*\mu$ on divisors of $n_0$
The extracted $M$ is coprime to $n_0$, so $\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$ is defined
Odd prime LTE and the 2-adic LTE variant apply in the stated hypotheses
Hensel lifting is applied only at simple roots (derivative not divisible by the prime)
uniqueness_certificate(s): Möbius inversion uniquely determines $f(n_0)$ and hence uniquely determines $t_2$, $t_p$, and $M$
The valuation profile of $M\pm 1$ together with 2-adic LTE and prime-power order lifting uniquely fixes the local orders and therefore fixes $r$ via CRT-lcm
Quadratic reciprocity determines the Legendre symbols needed for solvability, Hensel lifting fixes the prime-power root counts, and CRT fixes $s$; hence $r+s$ is unique

technique(s).name: Dirichlet_convolution_optional
technique(s).purpose: encode the hypothesis as a convolution equation

technique(s).name: mobius_inversion_optional
outputs: formula $f=F*\mu$ and a Möbius sum for $f(n_0)$

technique(s).name: multiplicative_functions
technique(s).purpose: factor the Möbius sum into a product and a linear bracket

technique(s).name: v_p_valuation
technique(s).purpose: extract $t_2=v_2(f(n_0))$ and $t_p=v_p(f(n_0))$ and form the unit $M$
outputs: integer $M$ coprime to $2p$ (and checked coprime to $q$)

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
technique(s).purpose: recombine local orders and root counts across components

technique(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
technique(s).purpose: decide quadratic residuosity via Legendre symbols

technique(s).name: Hensel_lifting
inputs: a simple square root modulo an odd prime
technique(s).purpose: lift square roots to prime powers and count them

theorem(s).name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE
theorem(s).role: controls $v_\ell(a^{\ell^m}-1)$ for odd primes and supports order lifting

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: supports the order-lcm relation and the multiplicativity of solution counts for coprime moduli

theorem(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
theorem(s).role: enables computation of Legendre symbols to decide quadratic residuosity

theorem(s).name: hensel_lemma
theorem(s).role: guarantees unique lifting of simple roots to prime powers

theorem(s).name: euler_theorem
theorem(s).role: guarantees existence of some $r>0$ with $M^r\equiv 1\pmod{n_0}$ when $\gcd(M,n_0)=1$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Divisor-sum transforms are inverted by Möbius inclusion-exclusion
Prime-power congruences are controlled by p-adic valuations and lifted in towers
Global constraints are reconstructed from local data by CRT

critical_trick(s): Squarefree collapse over three primes and uniform full-support evaluation gives a closed form for $f(n_0)$
Dual valuation extraction produces a unit $M$ that feeds both an order computation and a square-root count
2-adic LTE and odd-prime order lifting determine local orders, recombined by CRT-lcm
Quadratic reciprocity and Hensel lifting determine prime-power root counts, recombined by CRT

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Möbius inversion sums, multiplicative order, Legendre symbols, and modular square roots

task(s).name: independent_verification
task(s).reason: Recompute $f(n_0)$ by Möbius inversion, extract $t_2,t_5,M$, compute $r=\operatorname{ord}_{n_0}(M)$, and count square roots modulo $5^3\cdot 13^2$
